subject: Cloud Computing
title: Data Mesh: Decentralized Data Ownership
description: A fast breakdown of Data Mesh: Decentralized Data Ownership for builders.

hook.text: A central data team becomes a bottleneck. Every team waits in line for data access. Data mesh flips the model.

buildup.text: Data mesh gives each team ownership of their domain's data. They publish it as a product for others to consume.

discovery.text: Teams define schemas, SLAs, and quality standards for their data products — just like API contracts.

twist.text: Data mesh requires cultural change, not just technical change. Teams must think of data as a product, not a byproduct.

climax.text: Early adopters report faster data access and better quality. Critics say it creates data silos if done poorly.

punchline.text: Centralized data bottlenecks. Decentralized data democratizes.

quiz.question: What is the core idea behind data mesh?
options: Domain teams own their data and publish it as products for others
All data is stored in a single central database
Only the data engineering team can access or modify data
